generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Passive Data Transmission)
instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor component in the MABI MAX 100 first axis that generates torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω) outputs, but does not receive actuation commands from the digital twin. Operates within the physical twin system for high-precision manufacturing tasks."
]

instance GearboxTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Combination of gearwheel and cycloidal gearbox in the MABI MAX 100 first axis that processes motor torque (τ_M) and angular velocity (ω) to produce output torque (τ_G, τ_out) and angular velocity (ω_out). No direct actuation from digital twin operates in passive data transmission mode."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor shaft, providing real-time angular displacement data for backlash and positional uncertainty analysis in the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder mounted behind the cycloidal gearbox in the MABI MAX 100 first axis, providing real-time output angle data for friction analysis and positional accuracy verification."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderData
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller integrated current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor for torque derivation and dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time angular displacement data streamed from the motor encoder to the digital shadow via MQTT protocol for friction analysis and positional accuracy verification."
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time output angle data streamed from the secondary encoder to the digital shadow via MQTT protocol for backlash and positional uncertainty analysis."
]

instance CurrentSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
    base:desc "Input current (I_M) data streamed from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller to the digital shadow via MQTT protocol for torque derivation and dynamics modeling."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) and Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing the structural composition of the MABI MAX 100 first axis including motor, gearwheel, and cycloidal gearbox components with rotational/current flow definitions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLTool
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction, used as baseline for friction analysis in the MABI MAX 100 first axis."

]

    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model for the cycloidal gearbox in the MABI MAX 100 first axis, used to analyze static and kinetic friction components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model for the MABI MAX 100 first axis, capturing stiction and breakaway effects in gearbox dynamics."

]

    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for the cycloidal gearbox, used to analyze dynamic friction behavior under varying loads."

]

    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model for the MABI MAX 100 first axis, providing detailed characterization of friction dynamics including micro-sliding effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance MotorDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model representing motor dynamics (τ_M, ω_M) calculations using manufacturer-provided dynamics models for the permanent magnet synchronous motor in the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

instance GearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model representing gearbox dynamics (τ_G, ω_G) calculations incorporating gear ratios and friction effects for the cycloidal gearbox in the MABI MAX 100 first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool
]

    base:desc "Data calculation algorithm deriving torque (τ) values from measured inputs (I_M, encoder data) using manufacturer-specific dynamics models for the MABI MAX 100 first axis."

]

    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data stream of input current (I_M) measured by the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller, used as input for torque derivation and dynamics modeling."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorData

]

    DTDFVocab:enables StructuralModelingService
]

    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicsSimulationService
]

]

    DTDFVocab:enables TorqueDerivationService
    DTDFVocab:enables VelocityDerivationService
]

]

// C18: Environment
instance MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment containing the physical and digital components of the MABI MAX 100 first axis (base axis) including motor, gearbox, sensors, and digital shadow for friction analysis."
    base:contains MotorActuator
    base:contains GearboxTransmission
    base:contains MotorEncoder
    base:contains SecondaryEncoder
    base:contains CurrentSensor
]

// C19: Component
instance FirstAxisDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Purpose-specific digital shadow of the MABI MAX 100 first axis designed for friction analysis, incorporating SysML structural models, Simulink behavioral models, and real-time data assimilation from MQTT streams."
    base:isContainedIn MabiMax100FirstAxisEnvironment
    base:contains SysMLStructuralModel
    base:contains SimulinkTool
    base:contains MQTTProtocol
    base:contains DataProcessingEnabler
    base:contains AssetAdministrationShell
    base:contains VisualizationDashboard
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronizing with the physical system’s operational frequency. Data is processed at **high temporal resolution** (millisecond-level granularity) to enable real-time friction identification. The digital shadow updates dynamically with **live sensor data streams** (e.g., MQTT) and **simulation models** (e.g., Simulink), ensuring low-latency analysis for motion accuracy improvements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is structured as follows:
  - **Core Components**:
    - **Digital Shadow (FrictionShadow)**: Purpose-specific abstraction for friction analysis, incorporating **Purpose (FrictionDerivation)**, **Asset (Mabi)**, and **Processing components** (Simulation M, GW, GB).
    - **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders) and derived data (e.g., torque from dynamics models).
    - **Models**: Linked engineering (SysML), simulation (Simulink), and data calculation models.
  - **Data Flow**:
    1. Physical system → **MQTT streams** → Digital Shadow (data ingestion).
    2. Digital Shadow → **Processing components** (Simulink) → Simulated friction outputs.
    3. Unified dashboard → **Visualization** of deviations between measured and simulated data.
  - **Orchestration**: Centralized pipeline with **traceable data provenance**, enabling end-to-end analysis of friction effects on motion accuracy."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates during the **operational phase** of the physical system’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representations of the first axis. It does not model the **as-designed** (ideal) or **as-manufactured** phases but instead **continuously refines its models** based on real-time operational data to improve motion accuracy. The digital shadow is **dynamic and adaptive**, evolving alongside the physical system’s wear and usage patterns."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **measured vs. simulated data** (e.g., torque, angular velocity) to validate friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, etc.).
  - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Analysis of deviations between **reference data (measured)** and **simulated outputs** (e.g., ˆτ_M, ˆω_G) to assess model accuracy.
  - **Parameter Identification**: Continuous **adjustment of friction model parameters** during operation to account for **wear, temperature, and load variations**.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**: **Traceability** of data origins (e.g., MQTT channels) and processing steps (Simulink models) to ensure reproducibility.
  - **Error Sources**: Addressed through **dual encoder systems** (motor + secondary encoder) to mitigate backlash and positional uncertainty."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) is established via:
  - **Protocol**: **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for **real-time, low-latency data streaming** of sensor data (I_M, encoder outputs).
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data with **floating-point values** for torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω), structured as **DataTraces** in the digital shadow.
  - **Network Architecture**: **Unified dashboard** aggregates and visualizes data from the digital shadow, enabling **cross-model correlation** (e.g., SysML + Simulink). No explicit mention of cloud/edge deployment is provided; the system appears **locally integrated** with the CNC controller."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is **locally deployed** alongside the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller, leveraging **on-premise infrastructure** for real-time processing. The **dashboard visualization** is likely hosted on the same local network or integrated into the CNC system’s interface. No cloud-based deployment or distributed hosting is mentioned; the system relies on **local computation resources** (e.g., MATLAB Simulink runtime) for processing."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards:
  - **Modeling**: SysML (OMG standard) for **engineering models** (BDDs, IBDs).
  - **Asset Representation**: **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (IEC 62424) for semantic interoperability and data traceability.
  - **Simulation**: MATLAB Simulink (industry-standard tool) for **executable behavioral models**.
  - **Data Protocols**: MQTT (IETF standard) for **real-time data streaming**.
  The methodology also references **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** for structured digital shadow construction."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s security and safety considerations include:
  - **No Remote Actuation**: The digital shadow is **passive**; it does not execute operations remotely on the physical system, mitigating risks of unintended control interventions.
  - **Data Isolation**: Sensor data (e.g., MQTT streams) is **locally processed** without explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication). The system relies on the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.** controller’s inherent security for data integrity.
  - **Safety Constraints**: The robot’s **safety cell** restricts the working range to [-90°, 90°], ensuring operational limits are respected. However, **no explicit safety protocols** (e.g., fail-safes, emergency stops) are linked to the digital twin’s outputs.
  - **Human Safety**: The digital twin’s purpose is **analytical** (friction modeling), with no direct interaction with human operators during operation."
]
